title: mentha canadensis subsp. borealis
intro: Mentha canadensis subsp. borealis, also known as Canadian peppermint, is a subspecies of the mint plant native to North America. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a digestive aid and a natural antiseptic due to its high menthol and menthone content. The health benefits of Canadian peppermint include relief from nausea, indigestion, and headaches, as well as its ability to boost energy and improve respiratory function. The leaves of the plant can be used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly prepared by steeping the leaves in hot water or mixing them with a carrier oil.
